description: The provided image displays a "Check for updates" system icon and contains no discernible scientific content; all micrographic, spectroscopic, or plot details are unreadable from the current pixels. Based on the accompanying paper context for low-density FeMnAlC steels deciphered via machine learning, the intended figure likely supports the composition–microstructure correlation study of the validated Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C alloy. The text reports experimental characterization encompassing X-ray diffraction for FCC and κ-phase identification, scanning electron microscopy and electron probe microanalysis for homogeneous elemental distribution, transmission electron microscopy with selected-area electron diffraction for nanoscale intragranular κ-carbide precipitates, and electron backscatter diffraction confirming a recrystallized single-phase austenite matrix with an average grain size of 17 μm. Mechanical property data indicate a yield strength of 953 MPa, ultimate tensile strength of 1109 MPa, and total elongation of 42%. Furthermore, the machine learning workflow—employing KNN, ANN, CART, SVM, and random forest models—may be visualized. Because the scientific image is not visible, specific sub-panel identities, legends, peak positions, and microstructural features cannot be directly confirmed.
